name: host-orchestrator
description: Host-side orchestration of the full AFK pipeline. v4.0.0 (BREAKING): the pipeline engine is now a deterministic Workflow script (`workflows/prd-pipeline.js`, launched by the new `/prd-pipeline` command) — rules run as JS code, agents only implement/measure/resolve. Gate = pure `if` over validator numbers (generic ratchet: no metric worsens vs wave baseline, no before-green test goes red, diff must touch tests; invalid measurement is NEVER success). Integration-branch policy: creates `prd/<milestone>` (or `batch/<slug>`), issue PRs target it, base merged in before each wave, run ends with ONE draft PR to base for Leo's green button. All remote mutations serialized through idempotent check-then-act serializer agents (work-identity keyed, audit-logged). Capability tiers T0-T3 instead of model names (mapping only in repo config `model_map`); the launching session is the T0 orchestrator and pins each node's tier at design time. Native review fleet phase (partition → reviewers → judge → applier → gate). Resume via Workflow journal: `resumeFromRunId` only if nothing changed by hand, fresh run always safe. Spec: docs/SPEC-v4-workflow-engine.md (grilled 2026-07-16, validated by Piloto 1 ratchet-TS 2026-07-17: 6/6 criteria, 3 resumes without duplicated mutations). REMOVED in v4.0.0: `/afk-pipeline`, `/goal` wrapping, state.json, PROGRESS.md, cc-afk env-var scaffolding (only Bash/API timeouts survive). Kept as standalone surgical tools: `/parallel-implement-wave` (2-6 issues, parallel worktree subagents) and `/merge-orchestrate` (2-7 PRs, serial intent-aware merge). Custom subagents: `parallel-implementer` (TDD vertical-slice discipline, never pushes) and `merge-resolver` (5 no-regression criteria, recommends — serializer executes). Explicit invocation only; the Workflow tool's own opt-in gate doubles the marketplace rule.
